'''''Star Wars: Legacy of the Force''''' is a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] adult-fiction novel series from authors [[Aaron Allston]], [[Karen Traviss]], and [[Troy Denning]]. It includes nine volumes that were published by [[Del Rey]] from [[2006]] to [[2008]].

Set in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]–[[41 ABY]], the series chronicles the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] and the fall of [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], mentored by the returning villain [[Lumiya]]. ''Star Wars: Legacy of the Force'' also saw the return of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], [[Thrackan Sal-Solo]], and [[Natasi Daala]].

==Development==
When a nine-book series was first proposed in 2004, it was originally slated to be set in the [[Old Republic era]]. It is unknown when in the Old Republic era the series would have taken place, but on [[May 21]], [[2004]], [[Sue Rostoni]] said the following on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards|forums]] at [[StarWars.com]]:<ref name="SWArchive">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=173727&tstart=0|thread=Legacy of the Force series speculation (possible SPOILERS)|author=[[Sue Rostoni|Sremmuy]]|date=2004-05-21|time=09:47 AM}}</ref>
{{Quote|Shelly and I, and the authors, will be reading through all the comics and other material to decide where in time to set the series and what, if any, existing characters we want to use.|Sremmuy|<ref name="SWArchive" />}}
About a month later, Rostoni posted the following information in the thread:
{{Quote|Hi -- I haven't read all the pages of posts, but there's been a change.... We've reconsidered where to set this 9-book series and have decided that the old Old Republic era may not be the best place. We're now seriously considering post-NJO, something like 35 years after A New Hope. The thinking is that there is more opportunity here for substantial storylines using characters that people already have feelings about, and the outcome wouldn't be known at the start of things. We still want to play in the Old Old Republic time frame, and will try to get a trilogy there. I hope you all aren't disappointed - I'm trying to gauge whether it's best to give you NO information at all, or information (like this) that's subject to change.|Eeusu Estornii|<ref name="SWArchive2">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=173727&start=75|thread=Legacy of the Force series speculation (possible SPOILERS)|author=[[Sue Rostoni|Eeusu Estornii]]|date=2004-06-16|time=12:16 AM}}</ref>}}
==Continuity==
''Star Wars: Legacy of the Force'' has seen the re-emergence of several characters, both from the EU and from the films, who had until the series been untouched by EU literature for some time. Most notably the Dark Lady of the Sith Lumiya made her first appearance in a novel in ''Betrayal'', and Boba Fett, last seen in a cameo role in ''The Unifying Force'', re-entered the galactic scene in ''Bloodlines''. Other characters, such as Thrackan Sal-Solo, Gilad Pellaeon, and Ailyn Vel, as well as Natasi Daala, had their story-arcs brought to a conclusion.
